"Your greatest weakness will be the point at which Satan will try to tempt you, will try to win you; and if you have made yourself weak, he will add to that weakness. Resist him, and you will gain in strength. If he tempts you in another way, resist him again and he will become weaker. In turn, you become stronger, until you can say, no matter what your surroundings may be, 'Get thee behind me,…

The first weeks of Jesus' ministry were full of glorious successes. Luke says of the Savior's first missionary journey, "Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee: and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about." (Luke 4:14.) Yet, when Jesus returned to Nazareth and declared his Messiahship to his former friends and neighbors, the response was uniformly hostile.…

The Greek word translated "Galilee" is really "Judea." Jesus evidently taught in synagogues other than those in the Galilee. (Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the New Testament, 2: 279.)

The Greek word translated "Galilee" is really "Judea." Jesus evidently taught in synagogues other than those in the Galilee. (Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the New Testament, 2: 279.)

Saul on the road to Damascus. In the passage quoted above, Matthew sees fulfillment of a Messianic prophecy in Jesus' connection with Nazareth. We have no specific reference in biblical literature to prophets declaring that the Messiah would be a Nazarene, unless this is a paronomastic allusion to Isaiah 11:1. The common term Synagogue actually comes from the Greek language. It means meeting house, Bet Knesset, in Hebrew. Meeting houses existed before the Second Temple was destroyed, although there were few of them that have been uncovered archaeologically. Several Synagogues were mentioned in Jesus’ ministry. power to shut heaven, that it rain not. Twice in the temple, Christ made statements that invoked rage from the Jews, who, perceiving the words as blasphemy, immediately took up "stones to cast at him" (John 8:58-59; 10:30). This casting of stones is simply a case of stoning by pelting. I could not see how we could really celebrate an Easter without discussing the Lord Jesus Christ. Why even the devils know that Jesus is the Christ. On one occasion the devils came crying out and saving, “… Thou art Christ, the Son of God. And he rebuking them suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was the Christ.” (Luke 4:41.) On one occasion the devils came crying out and saving, “… Thou art Christ, the Son of God. And he rebuking them suffered them not to speak: for they knew that he was the Christ.” (Luke 4:41.) On another occasion “… the evil spirit answered and said,…

Christ led by the Spirit into the wilderness— Tempted of Satan—Preaches in Nazareth and Galilee.

The Greek word translated "Galilee" is really "Judea." Jesus evidently taught in synagogues other than those in the Galilee. (Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the New Testament, 2: 279.)
